mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return (t-stat < 1.5) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output that improves near-term earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of prolonged restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are non-negative in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows a negative or zero mean 60-day forward return over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger macroeconomic expansion that increases corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction that triggers negative earnings revisions and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where INDPRO drops >0.5% MoM.
